title: Electron beam emitting assembly
abstract: There is provided an electron beam emitting assembly comprising a cylindrical cathode element (32) and a current source (36), wherein an electrically conductive element (40, 94) connected to the current source (36) is positioned to contact a longitudinal axis (56) of the cathode element (32). A first portion of electrically conductive adhesive (60) is used to position wire (40) to contact the axis (56). A second portion of electrically conductive adhesive (62) is positioned part-way along the cylindrical cathode element (32), spaced apart from the first portion of electrically conductive adhesive (60), the second portion (62) establishing a second electrical connection with the current source (36).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An electron beam emitting assembly comprising a cylindrical cathode element and a current source, wherein an electrically conductive element connected to the current source is positioned to electrically contact a longitudinal axis of the cathode element. 2. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 1, wherein the electrically conductive element is a rod inserted into a negative electrode. 3. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the electrically conductive element is moveable axially. 4. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 1, wherein the electrically conductive element is made from Tungsten wire or foil strip. 5. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 4, wherein the Tungsten wire or foil strip has a cross-sectional diameter of around 200 pm 2 . 6. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 4 or claim 5, wherein the electrically conductive element is secured to one end of the cathode element by a first portion of electrically conductive adhesive so as to be positioned coaxially with the longitudinal axis of the cathode element. 7. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 6, wherein the first portion of electrically conductive adhesive is disc-like in shape having a diameter the same as or greater than the cylindrical cathode element. 8. An electron beam emitting assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the cylindrical cathode element and the electrically conductive element are held in fixed relationship to an electrically insulating casing. 9. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 8, wherein the casing is formed with a conduit aligned with the longitudinal axis of the cathode element, the 

electrically conductive element disposed within the conduit so as to align with the longitudinal axis of the cathode element. 10. An electron beam emitting assembly according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a second portion of electrically conductive adhesive is positioned part-way along the cylindrical cathode element, the second portion establishing an electrical connection with the current source. 11. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 10, wherein the second portion of electrically conductive adhesive is annular in shape. 12. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 11, wherein the second portion of electrically conductive adhesive is formed as a cup with an apertured base, the cathode element extending through the apertured base. 13. An electron beam emitting assembly according to claim 12, wherein the cup is located substantially within an electrically insulating casing.
